cadence.name: Weekly KPI Review
cadence.backlogExpectation: Every high-priority KPI regression becomes an explicit backlog item in the next review cycle.

rules.trigger: Completion rate in the active review window falls below 70%.
rules.target: Maintain at least 70% completion throughput in-window.
rules.recommendedAction: Create a backlog item to reduce blocked or aging work and track recovery in the next weekly review.

rules.trigger: Assignment acceptance falls below 80% for the review window.
rules.recommendedAction: Review rejection and pending-acceptance patterns, then schedule a dispatch tuning task.

rules.trigger: Job completion conversion from assignment events falls below 65%.
rules.target: Maintain at least 65% completion conversion from assignments.
rules.recommendedAction: Add a backlog item for recurring completion blockers and verify the outcome in the next review.

rules.trigger: Any completion SLA breach or breached escalation is observed in the review window.
rules.target: Zero breached completion SLAs in the active review cycle.
rules.recommendedAction: Open an SLA recovery backlog item and inspect the staffing, escalation, and handoff causes.

rules.trigger: Utilization exceeds 85% or any worker enters an overloaded state.
rules.target: Keep utilization below 85% and eliminate overloaded worker pockets.
rules.recommendedAction: Plan a workload rebalance or staffing adjustment item before the next review.

rules.trigger: High-confidence match rate falls below 75% or p95 decisioning latency exceeds 250 ms.
rules.target: Maintain at least 75% high-confidence matches and p95 latency at or below 250 ms.
rules.recommendedAction: Add a tuning task for ranking weights, inputs, or cache performance if the signal persists.

rules.trigger: Three or more denied or failed sensitive actions occur in 24 hours.
rules.target: Keep denied sensitive actions below 3 per 24-hour review window.
rules.recommendedAction: Create a governance follow-up item for role misuse, permission drift, or operator guidance issues.
